task:
Implement confidence-gated context injection for the KG agent - Issue 211 Improvement 1. HIGHEST IMPACT fix.

Problem: The synthesis prompt ALWAYS injects retrieved content, regardless of relevance. When vector similarity is low, retrieved sections are irrelevant noise that misleads Claude.

Changes needed:
1. Add CONTEXT_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.5 class constant to KnowledgeGraphAgent in wikigr/agent/kg_agent.py
2. In the query method, after _vector_primary_retrieve returns the tuple of kg_results and max_similarity, check if max_similarity is below CONTEXT_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD
3. If below threshold: skip all pack context injection - hybrid retrieval, enhancements, full synthesis. Instead call a minimal synthesis that tells Claude to answer from its own expertise. Return query_type as confidence_gated_fallback.
4. Add _synthesize_answer_minimal method that calls Claude with just the question and a note that the pack had no relevant content.
5. Add tests in tests/agent/test_kg_agent_core.py for: low similarity triggers fallback, high similarity runs normal pipeline, minimal synthesis is used correctly.
6. Update test_query_never_calls_llm_cypher in tests/agent/test_kg_agent_semantic.py - it uses distance=0.9 which means similarity=0.1 which now triggers confidence gating.

Expected impact: Eliminates ALL negative deltas on packs where training is already strong - go-expert at 100 pct, react-expert at 100 pct.

Implement cross-encoder reranking for improved retrieval precision - Issue 211 Improvement 3.

Problem: The bi-encoder BGE bge-base-en-v1.5 embeds queries and documents independently. Cross-encoders see BOTH together for much more accurate relevance.

Changes needed:
1. Create new file wikigr/agent/cross_encoder.py with CrossEncoderReranker class using cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-12-v2 at 33MB on CPU. Method: rerank with query, results, top_k=5 returns top-k results sorted by cross-encoder score with ce_score added to each dict.
2. In wikigr/agent/kg_agent.py __init__: add enable_cross_encoder=False parameter. When True and use_enhancements=True, instantiate CrossEncoderReranker.
3. In _vector_primary_retrieve: if cross_encoder is active, fetch 2x candidates from semantic_search, then rerank down to max_results.
4. sentence-transformers already includes CrossEncoder - no new dependencies needed.
5. Create tests/agent/test_cross_encoder.py: test reranking with mocked model, empty results, top_k filtering, ce_score added, graceful init failure.

Expected impact: +10-15 pct retrieval precision. Cost: ~50ms per rerank which is negligible vs 10-15s Opus synthesis.

description: Improvements 4+5: Multi-query retrieval and content quality scoring
task:
Implement multi-query retrieval and content quality scoring - Issue 211 Improvements 4 and 5.

Improvement 4 - Multi-Query Retrieval:
Problem: A single query embedding misses content phrased differently.
Add _multi_query_retrieve method with question and max_results=5 to KnowledgeGraphAgent that: generates 2 alternative phrasings via Claude Haiku claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, searches with all 3 queries, deduplicates by title keeping highest similarity, returns sorted results.
Add enable_multi_query=False parameter to __init__. Use in _vector_primary_retrieve when enabled.

Improvement 5 - Content Quality Scoring:
Problem: Short stubs and long explanations are treated equally. Stubs waste context and dilute useful content.
Add _score_section_quality method with content and question returning float 0.0-1.0: penalize sections under 20 words by returning 0.0, score by length 0.2-0.8 plus keyword overlap up to 0.2. Add CONTENT_QUALITY_THRESHOLD = 0.3 and STOP_WORDS frozenset.
Integrate in _build_synthesis_context to filter sections below threshold before including in synthesis.

Create tests/agent/test_retrieval_enhancements.py with tests for both features.

Expected impact: Multi-query +15-25 pct recall; quality scoring prevents noise injection.

description: Improvement 6: Fix broken URL lists for losing packs
task:
Fix broken and incomplete URL lists for the losing packs - Issue 211 Improvement 6.

For each pack, update data/packs/PACK/urls.txt:

1. langchain-expert: Verify URLs use python.langchain.com not old docs.langchain.com. Add comprehensive coverage of concepts, how-to guides, tutorials, and integrations sections.

2. openai-api-expert: Keep working platform.openai.com URLs. Add GitHub alternatives that bypass 403 blocks: openai-cookbook examples, openai-python SDK README, cookbook.openai.com pages, OpenAI Agents SDK.

3. vercel-ai-sdk: Keep working sdk.vercel.ai URLs. Add GitHub source URLs - vercel/ai repo README, core packages, content/docs MDX files for reliable text extraction.

4. llamaindex-expert: Expand from ~30 to ~70+ URLs. Add getting_started, understanding, optimizing subsections. Add module guides, agent/workflow docs, examples, API reference categories.

5. zig-expert: Add zig.guide as comprehensive third-party guide, language-basics and standard-library sections, ziglang.org/learn, community resources, GitHub repo.

Do NOT remove any working existing URLs. Keep same format with one URL per line and # comments.

description: Improvement 7: Eval question quality review for losing packs
task:
Review and improve eval questions for the 8 losing packs - Issue 211 Improvement 7.

For each packs data/packs/PACK/eval/questions.jsonl:

1. go-expert with training 100 pct and pack 94 pct: Questions may test knowledge Claude already knows perfectly. Ensure questions specifically test pack-provided content, not general Go knowledge.

2. llamaindex-expert: Ensure questions match the docs covered by urls.txt. Fix any terminology that differs from LlamaIndex documentation.

3. vercel-ai-sdk: Questions should use Vercel AI SDK-specific terminology. Ensure ground_truth answers reflect current SDK API.

4. zig-expert: Ensure coverage of core Zig concepts like comptime, allocators, error unions. Fix questions that test obscure features not in urls.txt scope.

5. bicep-infrastructure: Verify questions do not overlap with general Azure docs. Focus on Bicep-specific syntax and patterns.

6. langchain-expert: Update ground_truth for questions about deprecated APIs. Ensure terminology matches current python.langchain.com docs.

7. openai-api-expert: Update questions for current API including Responses API and new models. Ensure ground_truth reflects latest OpenAI documentation.

8. react-expert with training 100 pct and pack 98 pct: Questions should test React 19+ specific features like Server Components, use hook, Actions that are pack-value-add over Claude training.

Format: one JSON per line, preserve id, domain, difficulty, question, ground_truth, source fields exactly.
